title: quercus lanata var. incana
intro: Quercus lanata var. incana, also known as the Woolly Oak or Incane Oak, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the Quercus genus and native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds found in its leaves, bark, and acorns. The health benefits of Quercus lanata var. incana include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and supporting cardiovascular health due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies through decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
